name: lbenv

metadata.name: LbEnv
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)

documentation:
LbEnv
=====

LbEnv is a python package that contains a set of tools to prepare the LHCb environment to build and run applications.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.version.isValidVersion
documentation:
Check if the specified version number is a valid (reasonable) one for the
specified project.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.version.expandVersionAlias
documentation:
Given a project and a version, check if the version is an alias for an
explicit version (e.g. latest, reco19) and return the real version or the argument.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.version.versionKey
documentation:
For a version string with numbers alternated by alphanumeric separators,
return a tuple containing the separators and the numbers.

For example:
\>>> versionKey('1.2.3')
(1, '.', 2, '.', 3)
\>>> versionKey('v10r0')
('v', 10, 'r', 0)
\>>> versionKey('1.2-a')
(1, '.', 2, '-a')

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.version.LCGInfoName
documentation:
Return the name of the file containing the list of externals in an LCG
relese.

```pycon
>>> LCGInfoName('x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt')
'LCG_externals_x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt.txt'
```

functions.name: LbEnv.fixProjectCase
documentation:
Convert a project name to its canonical case, if known, otherwise return
the string unchanged.

```pycon
>>> fixProjectCase('gaudi')
'Gaudi'
>>> fixProjectCase('DAvinci')
'DaVinci'
>>> fixProjectCase('UnKnown')
'UnKnown'
```

documentation:
Return True if the string looks like a platform id.

```pycon
>>> isPlatformId('x86_64-centos7-gcc64-opt')

>>> isPlatformId('slc4_ia32_gcc34')
True
>>> isPlatformId('include')
False
```

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.lookup.walkProjectDeps
documentation:
Return a tuple ((project, version), rootdir, dependencies) for the
requested project and all its dependencies.

The dependencies are returned as a list of (project, version) and the list
can be altered to control which dependency to follow and which not.

Note that when walking through the dependencies a project may appear more
than once (for diamond dependency graphs).

modules.name: LbEnv.Utils.Temporary
documentation: create temporary structure for remote buids

classes.name: LbEnv.Utils.Temporary.TemporaryDirectory
documentation:
Create and return a temporary directory.  This has the same
behavior as mkdtemp but can be used as a context manager.  For
example:

    with TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        ...

Upon exiting the context, the directory and everything contained
in it are removed.
FIXME: backport from Python 3.2 (see http://stackoverflow.com/a/19299884)

classes.name: LbEnv.Utils.Temporary.TempDir

methods.name: LbEnv.Utils.Temporary.TempDir.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

'keep_var' is used to define which environment variable will prevent
the deletion of the directory.

The other arguments are the same as tempfile.mkdtemp.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.script.decodePkg
documentation:
Translate a string declaring a data package into a pair (package, version).

The user can specify the data package they want to use in different ways
and in all cases we need to return the package name with "hat" (also when
the hat is implied) and the version pattern.

```pycon
>>> decodePkg('Hat/Package')
('Hat/Package', '*')
>>> decodePkg('Package v7r*')
('Package', 'v7r*')
>>> decodePkg('Package v1r0 Hat')
('Hat/Package', 'v1r0')
>>> decodePkg('Hat/Package.v1r0')
('Hat/Package', 'v1r0')
>>> decodePkg('Hat.Package.v1r0')
('Hat/Package', 'v1r0')
>>> decodePkg('a b c d')
Traceback (most recent call last):
   ...
ValueError: too many words in package declaration 'a b c d'
```

documentation: Return any extra search path required by the project at 'projroot'.

Functions to be able to use the old SetupProject (CMT) from within
LbEnv.ProjectEnv.
(c) Copyright 2018 CERN                                                     #
This software is distributed under the terms of the GNU General Public      #
Licence version 3 (GPL Version 3), copied verbatim in the file "LICENSE".   #
In applying this licence, CERN does not waive the privileges and immunities #
granted to it by virtue of its status as an Intergovernmental Organization  #
or submit itself to any jurisdiction.                                       #

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.FindProjectVersions
documentation:
Given a project name, discovers all the matching project/versions using
the provided user_area and the the search_path (a list of directories).
Returns a list of tuples with (name, version, realname, basepath) where
  'name'     is the canonical name of the project (e.g. Gaudi, DaVinci)
  'version'  is the canonical version string (e.g. v2r3)
  'realname' is the name of the root directory of the project (e.g GAUDI/GAUDI_v21r6,
             LHCb_v28r3, DBASE)
  'basepath' is the directory in which 'realname' is based.
If there is no version the second entry is None.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.FindLCGForROOT
documentation:
Return the version of LCGCMT for the required version of ROOT.

@param versions: list of tuples like the one returned by FindProjectVersions

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.SortVersions
documentation:
Give a list of version numbers, return a list with only unique elements
ordered by version.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.SortVersionTuples
documentation: Similar to SortVersions, but take the list of versions returned by FindVersions.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.LatestVersion
documentation:
Extract the latest version from a list of version tuples
(output of FindProjectVersions).

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.VersionMatch
documentation:
Compare a version string with a pattern. The pattern can be
the exact match, a glob pattern or None (in which case matches a None version).

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility._GetVersionTuple
documentation: Extract the version tuple corresponding to version 'pattern'.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.getNightlyCMTPROJECTPATH
documentation:
Simple wrapper around GetNightlyCMTPROJECTPATH.
Returns the list of entries in the CMTPROJECTPATH of the slot of the nightly
build.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.smartExpandVarsPath
documentation:
Expand the environment variables in a path. If the variable in the entry of
the path is a path itself, one entry is added for each element of the
variable. (The code is more or less inspired by posixpath.py)
Note: replaces only the first variable in each entry.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.uniq
documentation: Returns a list of unique elements in the passed iterable.
Utility functions

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.removeall
documentation: Recursively remove directories and files.

functions.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.expandAllVars
documentation:
Expand all variables in the environment dict.

```pycon
>>> env = {'MYDATA': '${DATA}/subdir', 'DATA': '/main/path'}
>>> expandAllVars(env)['MYDATA']
'/main/path/subdir'
```

methods.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.GetNightlyCMTPROJECTPATH.value
documentation: Return the CMTPROJECTPATH.

SAX content handler to extract the CMTPROJECTPATH from lcg nightly build
configuration for a given slot/day.
Usage:
from xml.sax import parse
getter = GetNightlyCMTPROJECTPATH("lhcb2","Mon")
parse(filename, getter)
print(getter.value())

methods.name: LbEnv.ProjectEnv.compatibility.SetupProject.main
documentation:
Main entry point to SetupProject script.

Calls parse_args, then the 'run' method, profiling it if requested.
